### Post by cbilljones on 2007-06-30
I've always been able to mount ntfs no problem but im a little confused now. I formated the drive to ext2 with gparted and just need to know how to mount, thanks:)

### Post by cbilljones on 2007-06-30
heres my fstab, it is still seeing it as ntfs, do i just need to edit this?

# /etc/fstab: static file system information.
#
#  -- This file has been automaticly generated by ntfs-config -- 
#
# <file system> <mount point>   <type>  <options>       <dump>  <pass>

proc /proc proc defaults 0 0
# Entry for /dev/sda1 :
UUID=a353c62c-b7c1-43d8-b538-9379d44a791a / ext3 defaults,errors=remount-ro 0 1
# Entry for /dev/sda5 :
UUID=cd6728c6-e9ff-47dc-9e52-5d58dd5f91b5 none swap sw 0 0
/dev/scd0 /media/cdrom0 udf,iso9660 user,noauto 0 0
/dev/sdb1 /media/Shared ntfs umask=222,utf8 0 0

### Post by cbilljones on 2007-07-01
still having trouble on this, i tried a few things but no luck. The drve is formatted to ext3, heres my currant fstab

# /etc/fstab: static file system information.
#
#  -- This file has been automaticly generated by ntfs-config -- 
#
# <file system> <mount point>   <type>  <options>       <dump>  <pass>

proc /proc proc defaults 0 0
# Entry for /dev/sda1 :
UUID=a353c62c-b7c1-43d8-b538-9379d44a791a / ext3 defaults,errors=remount-ro 0 1
# Entry for /dev/sda5 :
UUID=cd6728c6-e9ff-47dc-9e52-5d58dd5f91b5 none swap sw 0 0
/dev/scd0 /media/cdrom0 udf,iso9660 user,noauto 0 0
/dev/sdb1 /storage ext3 defaults 0 0

Thanks for any help you can offer

---

### Post by logos34 on 2007-07-01
Did you create the mount point for it?

I'd use /media/storage instead:

**sudo mkdir /media/storage**

Change fstab to match

Then,

**sudo mount -a**

---

### Post by cbilljones on 2007-07-01
Thanks for the response, i think i had it before and just needed a full reboot:(, i just dont have write permissions, is it just a simple command?

---

### Post by logos34 on 2007-07-01
> **cbilljones said:**
> Thanks for the response, i think i had it before and just needed a full reboot:(, i just dont have write permissions, is it just a simple command?

If you had it before and it mounts at /storage, and your username is, say, 'cbill', then try this:

> [B]sudo chown -R cbill:cbill /storage
sudo chmod -R 755 /storage[/B]

---

### Post by cbilljones on 2007-07-01
Thanks:p i had used your advise before so it was actually;

sudo chown -R cbill:cbill /media/storage
sudo chmod -R 755 /media/storage

I think i would have been OK on my own if i would have done full reboots initially instead of just restarting X, does that make sense?

---
